name: Puzzles of the World - Early Access

reviews.review_description: I just finished building my Yasaka Pagoda in VR, and it feels amazing to see it standing tall! The feeling of placing each piece yourself is oddly therapeutic, and I can’t wait for more puzzles to come. Great for anyone who loves building, exploring, or just zoning out in VR.

reviews.review_title: I gave it two stars/but the scenery was
reviews.review_description: Where the scenery was beautiful and you could change from day to night and then it changes you￼in different areas inside of the game when it came to putting the puzzle together you should’ve been  able to fit the board on the table solidly on the board to where I would not move. ￼ When you go to put the puzzle pieces on the frame they should have clicked in place when you put it by the pieces that it connected to. Mind you I understand that you were trying to develop this app where you would actually be like putting the puzzle together with your hand.  But even then, it shouldn’t have been so hard where the board kept moving and sticking into your hand and the puzzle pieces that you were trying to put on wouldn’t snap in place or it just should’ve been a little bit more easier and relaxing I was frustrated and I just stopped where I would go back and look at the scenery that puzzle. Please improve it. ￼

developer_response.body:
Hi SweetMusic4me, thank you for taking the time to share such detailed feedback! We’re glad you enjoyed the POV and Time of Day switching!

We’re making changes how the board behaves and how pieces align and settle, with the goal of making assembly feel more stable and intuitive. We’re also working on adding controller support coming very soon.

Thanks again for the honest review. Please look forward to new updates over the coming days and weeks!

reviews.review_description:
I spend hours building puzzles in VR and wishlisted this the moment i saw it.  The 3d wooden models is unique, the graphics are great, I love that you can move to different preselected areas in that scene and change to day or night, the step by step instruction manual is great.  But these things kept me from a higher rating, please take this a constructive feed back from someone who would love this game.
First hand tracking only, hand tracking rarely works well for me and makes some interactions more difficult, please allow the choice to use controllers, second is playing seated is near impossible, yes you can raise the table up and down but you cant bring it closer to you or turn the table to get pieces on the far side of the table, the table spawns out of arms reach, maybe add a force grab gesture to grab pieces or more table movement options, I found myself standing to get each piece, plus all the menus you have to stand to reach made what should be relaxing sit down activty a bit tiring.  And last I did find the puzzles a bit too easy, just get the piece near one of several places that piece shape goes and it jumps into place. Consider adding some difficulty levels to make it more challenging.  I do love the concept and hope you consider making some quality of life changes.

developer_response.body:
Hi Ellehcim99, thank you so much for the constructive feedback and for sharing what you enjoyed!

Controller support is coming soon, and we’re also working on improving comfort and accessibility so seated play and menu interaction feel much more natural. On puzzle difficulty, and we’re looking into adding more challenging versions of puzzles with a larger number of pieces.

We’ll be rolling out improvements in upcoming updates, so we hope you continue to play and enjoy our game!

reviews.review_title: Great game, but has a bit to go
reviews.review_description: The game is great! i enjoyed the first puzzle and am starting the second, however, i have a few iffs about gameplay. atm the hand tracking is very difficult. it seems to pause, freeze and sometimes interact/uninteract when im using it, it would be nice to have a controller feature so you can use controllers, as sometimes when i bumped into my controllers it immediately stopped my hands. other than that, its a very fun game! cant wait to see what comes of it in the future, as this is a very unique and awesome game. definetly felt like i was building a real wooden model kit!

developer_response.body:
Hi NateslPlayzzz, thank you for the detailed feedback and for trying both puzzles. This will definitely help us in improving Puzzles of the World through the Early Access period.

We’re working hard on a bunch of improvements over the coming weeks, with controller support coming very soon. We’re also polishing the building experience to make interactions feel smoother and more consistent.

Thanks again and keep an eye out for new updates, puzzles, and locations soon!

reviews.review_description: Deeply relaxing in cozy environments, loved the art style! Also, learned cool stuff while building the miniatures.

I really loved the idea of the game, but having hand tracking as the only option is a massive downfall. It would be so much easier if you could use your controllers to move the pieces/board and pages. It’s super glitchy.

developer_response.body: Hi SockoA24, thanks for the review! We just updated the game to include controller support and a distance grab feature, along with a few other things. We hope you can give a try and leave us additional feedback!

reviews.review_description:
VR puzzle games are a difficult genre to stand out in, but Puzzles of the World succeeds brilliantly—and even feels like it’s creating its own sub-genre. The environments are colorful, thoughtfully designed, and a joy to spend time in.

What truly sets the game apart is how the puzzles involve building meaningful structures, such as a Venetian gondola or a Japanese pagoda. These construction-based puzzles feel rewarding, intuitive, and satisfying to complete.

Along the way, you also learn interesting facts about real-world locations and cultures, adding an educational layer that blends naturally with the gameplay rather than interrupting it. It’s calm, engaging, and genuinely enriching.

This is a beautifully crafted experience with a lot of heart. I sincerely hope the developers continue to expand it with new locations and puzzles in the future.

A great addition to any quality VR library. Well done. 👏
Installed, uninstalled, installed...several times. This app does NOT work. No action is possible beyond the welcoming scren. Please fix and Ill change my review score!
no hands so can not click to start a puzzle one star .

developer_response.body: Hi soulreaperGSXR, thank you for downloading and for your feedback! Puzzles of the World only supports Hand Tracking at the moment, which is why you were unable to start a puzzle. We're working on adding controller support very soon. In the meantime, you can play by setting the controllers aside and using your hands.

reviews.review_description: Beautiful game! The scenery is breathtaking. With day/night modes, and change of location. The music in Venice was extremely relaxing. The puzzle building aspect felt like the real thing. Can't wait to see what other levels and packs come after!

developer_response.body: Hi Bbt636, thank you so much for the kind words! We’re really happy you're enjoying the visuals and music. Please keep an eye out for our next puzzles and locations coming soon!

reviews.review_description:
It’s got some nice ideas but it requires hand tracking which I never use, for a reason. It’s buggy, glitchy and over all frustrating to use. Trying to just pick up pieces was so irritating. Also, the platform that the pieces sat on was so far away from me, I had to reach really far to pick them up only for the hand tracking to barely work at all. 

I like the idea, but the execution needs a lot of work.

Loads up but can't get past loaded scree
n  can't click on play such a shame looks good game I hope they sort it soon

developer_response.body: Hi Jools109, thank you for checking out our game, and we’re glad it caught your eye! Puzzles of the World currently supports Hand Tracking only, which is why you may not be able to start a puzzle if you were using controllers. We’re working on adding controller support very soon. In the meantime, please set the controllers aside and use your hands to play.

reviews.review_description: Can't input on the title screen. No hands.

developer_response.body: Hi Couget, thanks so much for downloading and for letting us know! Puzzles of the World currently supports Hand Tracking only, which is likely why you couldn’t see your hands in the Main Menu or start a level. We’re working on adding controller support very soon. In the meantime, please set the controllers aside and use your hands to play.

reviews.review_description: Does load but no hands showing freezes on main page

developer_response.body: Hi davesureshot, thank you for your feedback! Puzzles of the World currently works only with Hand Tracking, which is why you were unable to select the level in the Main Menu. We're working on adding controller support very soon but in the meantime you can play with your hands.

reviews.review_description: This could be a great game. My problem is the hand tracking is awful. Attaching pieces and flipping the user manual is often frustrating. I played for 15 minutes and could not finish step one without wanting to my Metaquest through a wall. If all this was smooth this would be the most relaxing game on Metaquest. I'm not giving up, I'm hoping this will eventually get fixed and will be able to play. As it is now,I wouldn't pay a dime.

I’ve always loved wooden puzzles, and experiencing them in VR is amazing. The relaxing music, beautiful views, and the satisfying feel of placing each piece by hand make it so immersive. Bring on more puzzles!
I built the Pagoda in the heart of Kyoto with some chill music in the background. What more do you need for a relaxing evening.. maybe just more puzzles.
You slowly build little 3D landmarks and it’s actually pretty relaxing. Not stressful at all.
I like slowly putting the little scenes together,it feels calm and relaxing, and the environments look nice too.
Its not complicated, but its a nice way to relax and build something piece by piece!|

I really love the idea and have already played puzzling places a lot, which shares a similar vibe and gameplay. But I see some fundamental problems with this game. First of all, I ran into a bug where during the tutorial I pressed the "switch scenes"-button and then the tutorial was gone, but i didnt have the pieces yet, so I couldnt progress. I had to press the switch-scenes-button until I reached the original position again to get the tutorial back. I dont really get the purpose of the table, as neither the pieces nor the buttons move with it. For me the table was just an annoying obstacle that I would sometimes accidently grab. The biggest problem for me is the accessibility. The pieces are spread like a meter in front of you, so if you are sitting or have a small playspace, you will not reach them. There is also no distance grab, which I think is a must for this type of game. I would spread the pieces out in like 180° around the player, because right now its not really using the reachable space efficiently. The instructions are very hard to see. I would just put one instruction on the whole page and make it flip automatically. For me it felt like you dont really figure out which piece goes where, you just figure out which piece is currently the next and place it somewhere close to the main thing and it snaps in place. I think it would be better if there was a highlight to show you where the piece goes while you hold it very close. You could even highlight the pieces you need next and ditch the whole instruction book. That way you would also not see in advance what you are building, which would make it more fun in my opinion. I like the idea of the little information pages, but I found myself skipping them because I wanted to continue to puzzle. I think they should be separate from the instructions, like projected in the scene and a narrator reads the text.

developer_response.body:
Hi Searine, thanks for the detailed feedback. The tutorial bug you described shouldn't be happening, and we’re reviewing that flow so the tutorial can continue even if you switch scenes. 

We also hear you on accessibility for seated play and smaller spaces, and on the table and instruction readability. Both of these aspects are key Early Access focuses that we will be improving very soon.

Thanks again for the honest critique. We’re incorporating changes like these in upcoming updates, stay tuned!

reviews.review_description: Love wooden puzzles, so enjoyed the experience. Thanks
